source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §General Data (p.7)
text: ES44DC General Data. Manufacturer: GE Transportation. BNSF assignment range: road numbers 7650–7799. Engine: GEVO-12 (12-cylinder, four-stroke, turbocharged), 4500 horsepower gross. Traction: six DC traction motors, model 5GEB752AH, one per axle (Co-Co, six-axle). Length over couplers: 73 ft 2 in. Maximum operating speed: 75 mph. Air-brake system: Wabtec CCBII electronic air brake. Electrically released parking brake (EPB) at the trailing truck. Fuel capacity: 5300 US gal. Cooling: hydraulically driven radiator fans. Auto Engine Start/Stop (AESS) standard.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Master Controller (p.19)
text: Master Controller. Combined Power/Reverser handle has positions: REVERSE / OFF / FORWARD (reverser) and IDLE / NOTCH 1–8 / DYN BRAKE (power). The reverser is mechanically interlocked with the throttle: power notches are blocked unless the reverser is in FORWARD or REVERSE; conversely the reverser cannot be moved off OFF unless the throttle is at IDLE. In trail/dead-in-train operation place the reverser at OFF and the isolation switch at START/STOP; only the lead unit's controller commands traction. Removal of the reverser handle locks the controller.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §SDIS Operation Screen (p.22)
text: SDIS (Smart Display Information System) Operation Screen. Top status bar shows ER (equalizing reservoir), BP (brake pipe), BC (brake cylinder), and MR (main reservoir) pressures in psi, plus throttle notch and tractive effort/braking effort in lb. Alarm bar across the bottom of the screen aggregates active faults; touching an alarm opens the detail page. Pressure target lines on the gauge indicate normal range (BP 90 psi, MR 130–140 psi). Tractive effort indicator color: green = within limits, amber = wheelslip/derate active, red = motor cut-out.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §CCBII Air Brake (p.35)
text: CCBII Electronic Air Brake. Electronic Brake Valve (EBV) positions: REL (release / running), MIN (minimum service reduction 6 psi), FS (full service ~26 psi reduction), SUP (suppression — clears penalty/PCS), CS (continuous service — incremental), EMER (emergency, vents BP to atmosphere). The target display shows the commanded BP pressure. Emergency application automatically applies sand on the lead unit until the train stops or 30 seconds elapse. Independent brake handle is separate from the automatic; pressing the BAIL-OFF releases independent during automatic application on the locomotive.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Cut-Out Cocks (p.39)
text: Brake-System Cut-Out Cocks. Main reservoir cut-out: under the cab on the engineer's side. Brake pipe cut-out cocks: at the A-end and B-end pilots. Actuating (A&R) cock and dead-engine feature cock: in the equipment rack adjacent to the CCBII. Set the dead-engine cock IN before towing dead-in-train so the BP charges the brake cylinders directly through the dead-engine feature.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Air Compressor (p.47)
text: Air Compressor. Motor-driven, two-stage reciprocating compressor charging the main reservoirs. ARPS (Air Reservoir Pressure Sensor) governs cut-in (~125 psi) and cut-out (~140 psi). MR safety valves: low-pressure stage 75 psi, high-pressure stage 180 psi. Compressor oil pressure is monitored; low pressure causes a compressor cut-out with SDIS alarm 'COMPRESSOR FAIL'.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Alerter, Alarm Bell, Ground Cut-Out (p.55)
text: Alerter. Resets on any control input (throttle change, brake, horn, alerter pushbutton). Default reset window is speed-dependent: ~25 s at track speed, longer at low speed. A penalty brake applies if not reset; suppression (EBV to SUP) clears the penalty. Alarm Bell sounds for safety alerts and penalty applications. Ground Cut-Out (Ground Fault Detection): a ground current >0.93 A removes traction entirely; 0.5–0.93 A derates to limit further damage; <0.5 A is within tolerance. Persistent ground faults require shop attention to the main alternator, traction motor leads, or DBR resistor grid.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Motor Cut-Out / HP Available (p.58)
text: Traction Motor Cut-Out and HP Available. With 0, 1, or 2 motors cut out, full 4500 HP remains available. With 3 motors cut out, the locomotive derates (approximately 2/3 HP). With 4 or 5 motors cut out, severe derate and tractive-effort limit imposed. With 6 motors cut out (all), no horsepower is available — the locomotive must be towed.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Oil/Water Temp & Pressure (p.59)
text: Engine Protection Thresholds. Engine oil temperature: derate begins at 185°F, engine shutdown at 212°F. Water (coolant) temperature: derate at 212°F, shutdown at 230°F. Engine oil pressure: low-pressure alarm trips below a speed-dependent minimum (≈10 psi at idle, ≈40 psi at notch 8); sustained low oil pressure causes engine shutdown. Hot Engine alarm is latched until reset at the SDIS after temperatures return within range.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Wheelslip & Sanding (p.60)
text: Wheelslip Detection and Sanding. The control system detects asynchronous (single-axle slip) and synchronous (all-axle) wheel slip via axle speed differentials. Automatic sand is applied when slip is detected, when emergency brake is applied (lead unit), and on a manual sand request. Synchronous slip retards power until adhesion is recovered. Excessive slip events log SDIS alarm 'WHEELSLIP EXCESS'; persistent slip on one axle suggests a contaminated rail or a failing traction motor on that truck.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Starting Engine + Cold Weather (p.73)
text: Starting the Engine. Verify isolation switch in START/STOP; reverser handle OFF; throttle IDLE; battery knife switch closed. Press PRELUBE — the pre-lube pump runs 20–30 s; cranking is blocked until the cycle completes. Then press START. After fire, the engine runs at low idle. In cold weather (<40°F) the engine remains derated until oil and water temps come up; the BCCB (battery charging contactor breaker) controls charge rate. Do not load notch above 4 until oil temp >100°F.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Before Moving / Brake Pipe Leakage Test (p.75)
text: Before Moving — Brake Pipe Leakage Test. With the train fully charged at 90 psi BP and the EBV at FS (full service), the brake pipe leakage shall not exceed 5 psi per minute per 49 CFR 232.205(c)(1) and 232.209(b)(1). Test is conducted with the independent released so leakage cannot be masked by locomotive brake-cylinder bleed. If leakage exceeds 5 psi/min the locomotive is bad-ordered for the brake system until the leak is found and corrected.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Dead-In-Train Setup (p.81)
text: Dead-In-Train Setup. Place reverser at OFF and isolation switch at START/STOP. Open the MR drain cocks and drain main reservoirs to below 25 psi. Set the dead-engine feature cock IN so that brake pipe air feeds locomotive brake cylinders through the dead-engine reducing valve. Close all unnecessary breakers. Ensure parking brake is RELEASED before pulling — the EPB will otherwise drag and overheat the trailing-truck shoes.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §Electric Parking Brake (p.81)
text: Electric Parking Brake (EPB). Located on the trailing truck. APPLY: hold APPLY switch until SDIS shows 'EPB APPLIED'. RELEASE: hold RELEASE switch until 'EPB RELEASED' shown. MANUAL: rotate the hand-wheel under the side platform clockwise to apply, counter-clockwise to release — used when the EPB motor is faulted. Fault recovery: on 'EPB FAULT' alarm, attempt one APPLY/RELEASE cycle; if the fault persists, use the hand-wheel and tag the EPB motor for shop replacement.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — §AESS Auto Engine Start/Stop (p.125)
text: AESS — Auto Engine Start/Stop. The engine will auto-stop when all of these conditions are met simultaneously for 10 continuous minutes: (1) reverser centered (OFF); (2) throttle at IDLE; (3) independent brake fully applied with BC > 22 psi; (4) main reservoir > 125 psi; (5) battery voltage > 65 V; (6) ambient air > 40°F (cold-weather inhibit); (7) AESS Suspend not active. Auto-start triggers on any of: low battery, low MR pressure, low water temp, throttle out of idle, or reverser out of OFF. AESS Suspend is set from the SDIS and inhibits both auto-stop and auto-start. SDIS displays 'AESS — ENGINE WILL STOP' 60 seconds before shutdown so the crew can override.

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — Tractive Effort Reference Table
text: Tractive Effort Reference (ES44DC, six 5GEB752AH DC traction motors). Starting tractive effort at 25% adhesion: ~142,000 lb. Continuous tractive effort at minimum continuous speed (~11 mph): ~109,000 lb. Notch-1 tractive effort: ~17,000 lb. Notch-8 tractive effort falls along the constant-horsepower curve from ~109,000 lb at 11 mph to ~22,000 lb at 65 mph. Tractive effort is automatically derated by the wheelslip controller and by motor cut-out; see §Motor Cut-Out (p.58).

source_label: GE ES44DC Operating Manual GEJ-6915 — Dynamic Braking Effort Reference Table
text: Dynamic Braking Effort Reference (ES44DC). Dynamic braking is extended-range; the DBR resistor grid dissipates traction-motor regenerated current. Maximum dynamic braking effort at minimum effective speed (~8 mph): ~96,000 lb. Effort falls along the constant-power curve to ~28,000 lb at 60 mph. Dynamic brake is automatically blended out below ~5 mph. Overheating of the DBR grid will trip the DBR contactor and remove dynamic brake until the grid cools.
